steps.title: Substitute the input
steps.content:
To find $f(1)$, substitute $1$ for every $x$ in the function. This evaluates the function at the requested input:
$$f(1)=3(1)^3+\frac{1}{4}(1)^2-1$$

steps.title: Simplify the value
steps.content:
Evaluate the powers first, then combine the whole-number terms before adding the fraction:
$$f(1)=3+\frac{1}{4}-1$$
$$f(1)=2+\frac{1}{4}=\frac{9}{4}$$
The answer is A.
